results.reason: Availability component is +1.0 when all healthy and 0.0 when one service is unhealthy.
Latency penalty matches formula across below/at/above-100ms and mixed-average cases.
Downtime component is -5.0 when 1, 2, or 3 services are down (applied once per step).
Efficiency component is 0.0 for NoOp and -0.1 for non-NoOp action types 1-4.
Safety penalty is -10.0 for restart on healthy service and 0.0 for restart on unhealthy service.
In a multi-penalty scenario, total reward equals the arithmetic sum of all five components.
After bad_config injection, rollback recovers db by step 2 (within 3 steps), reward improves (0.0 -> 0.9), and rollback has no safety penalty.
Restarting a healthy service yields lower reward than NoOp on the same healthy state (1.0 vs -9.1).
